title: Orthotic Support Worker
how_to_become: Apply for a vacancy on the NHS recruitment website or do a Modern Apprenticeship.
description: Orthotic support workers work with orthotists to design, make, and fit orthotic devices for patients. Orthoses are artificial or mechanical aids that provide support or assistance to a weakened part of the body, made from titanium, thermoplastics, leather, or carbon fibre.
role_overview: As an orthotic support worker, you'll use the orthotist's designs and specifications to make orthoses using the most suitable materials. You'll also be responsible for maintaining and repairing them, working with people of all ages with conditions including arthritis, stroke, cerebral palsy, spina bifida, and scoliosis.

entry_requirements.overview: A good standard of education is needed. There are no specific entry requirements.

work_experience: Work placements and volunteering can provide healthcare experience and help when applying for a new job with NHSScotland.

education_and_training.mandatory_induction: Mandatory Induction Standards must be completed within 3 to 6 months of employment.

additional_qualifications: SVQ Healthcare Support (Clinical) at SCQF level 7
Professional Development Award (PDA) in Rehabilitation Technologies

career_progression.overview: Experience as a healthcare support worker can support entry to university to become a registered orthotist.

chunks.text: To become an orthotic support worker, apply for a vacancy on the NHS recruitment website or do a Modern Apprenticeship in Engineering.

Orthotic support workers work with orthotists to design, make, and fit orthoses — artificial or mechanical aids providing support to weakened body parts, made from titanium, thermoplastics, leather, or carbon fibre.

Orthotic support workers use the orthotist's designs to make orthoses, and maintain and repair them. They work with people of all ages with conditions including arthritis, stroke, cerebral palsy, spina bifida, and scoliosis.
No specific entry requirements. Useful school subjects include Science, Engineering Science, English, and Maths.
Additional qualifications include SVQ Healthcare Support (Clinical) at SCQF level 7 and Professional Development Award (PDA) in Rehabilitation Technologies.
Experience as an orthotic support worker can support entry to university to become a registered orthotist.
